output:
I’m treating this as an end-to-end deployment and verification pass. I’ll inspect the repo for the Docker stack and health checks first, then I’ll provision the environment, start containers, and verify each service before I report back.I found the shared observability stack and a dirty worktree with unrelated changes, so I’m avoiding edits and focusing on runtime operations only. Next I’m reading the compose definition and any stack/status guidance, then I’ll bring the stack up and verify service health from both Docker and HTTP.The compose file is narrower than the status code expects: it defines `victoria-logs`, `victoria-metrics`, and `otel-collector`, but no `victoria-traces` service. I’m checking the local OTEL config and current harness state now so I can deploy the right stack and report that mismatch explicitly instead of masking it.The local state file doesn’t carry an active compose path here, so I’m using the root `docker-compose.harness.yml` as the deployment target. I also confirmed the declared services are exactly three, which means the trace backend mismatch is in config/template logic rather than in the compose plan itself.There’s already a long-lived shared `codeharness` stack on this machine with the full four-service topology, including Jaeger on `16686`. I’m switching to that as the canonical deployment target because it matches the harness’ expected runtime better than the repo-root compose, and I’m about to reconcile the exact stack path and then re-run `up -d` plus endpoint checks.I have the canonical shared stack file now. I’m re-applying the deployment with `docker compose up -d` against `~/.codeharness/stack/docker-compose.harness.yml`, then I’ll verify the four stack services from Docker state and live endpoints.The stack is up, the shared network has all four containers attached, and the public service endpoints are responding. I’m doing one last pass on the shared collector config and recent container logs to confirm there isn’t a silent backend wiring issue before I finalize the deployment report.<deploy-report>
status: PASS_WITH_WARNING

deployed_at: 2026-04-08
target_stack: codeharness shared observability stack
compose_file: ~/.codeharness/stack/docker-compose.harness.yml
provisioning:
- `docker compose -p codeharness-shared -f ~/.codeharness/stack/docker-compose.harness.yml up -d`
- network `codeharness-shared-net` present with 4 attached containers
- volumes present: `codeharness-shared_victoria-logs-data`, `codeharness-shared_victoria-metrics-data`

containers:
- `codeharness-shared-victoria-logs-1` — Up
- `codeharness-shared-victoria-metrics-1` — Up
- `codeharness-shared-victoria-traces-1` — Up
- `codeharness-shared-otel-collector-1` — Up

urls:
- logs: http://localhost:9428
- metrics: http://localhost:8428
- traces_ui: http://localhost:16686
- traces_collector: http://localhost:14268
- otel_http: http://localhost:4318
- otel_grpc: localhost:4317

credentials:
- No authentication configured for these local endpoints
- No username/password required

health:
- `victoria-logs` — `GET /health` => HTTP 200, body `OK`
- `victoria-metrics` — `GET /health` => HTTP 200, body `OK`
- `victoria-traces` — `GET /` => HTTP 200, Jaeger UI served
- `victoria-traces` — `GET /api/services` => HTTP 200, returned service list
- `otel-collector` HTTP receiver — `GET /` => HTTP 404, which confirms the listener is reachable on `:4318`
- `otel-collector` gRPC receiver — TCP port `4317` reachable
- recent container logs show normal startup; no active crash/restart loop detected

warning:
- The repo-local [docker-compose.harness.yml](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/docker-compose.harness.yml) is not the canonical deployed stack and only defines 3 services.
- The live shared stack at [~/.codeharness/stack/docker-compose.harness.yml](/Users/ivintik/.codeharness/stack/docker-compose.harness.yml) is the correct 4-service deployment used by codeharness.

result:
- Docker environment is provisioned
- All stack containers are running
- All service endpoints required by the harness are reachable
</deploy-report>
